concerns.text: The plan assumes a 'dense depth baseline' is either pre-computed in the RealEstate10K release or can be generated on-the-fly via a CPU-optimized model (MiDaS) to ensure a fair comparison. RealEstate10K does not provide pre-computed dense depth maps. Generating them on-the-fly using MiDaS (even CPU mode) for 200 video sequences within the 6-hour total pipeline limit is infeasible; MiDaS inference is computationally heavy and would likely exceed the time budget, invalidating the 'CPU-tractable' hypothesis. The plan must either secure a pre-computed baseline from a different source or redefine the baseline comparison to avoid on-the-fly dense generation.

The plan cites a specific HuggingFace URL (https://huggingface.co/datasets/DavidYan2001/RealEstate10K/resolve/main/dataset/test.tar.gz) for the dataset. While the resolver guarantees URL reachability, the plan assumes this specific user-uploaded repository contains the full, stratifiable RealEstate10K test set with necessary metadata. The official RealEstate10K is typically hosted by the original authors or a verified HuggingFace org (e.g., 'google-research-datasets'). Relying on a user-uploaded mirror (DavidYan2001) risks data integrity, version drift, or missing metadata required for the 'motion magnitude' stratification (FR-001). The plan should cite the canonical source or verify the mirror's completeness against the official release.
